title: BUG: Add delimiters to NameObject.renumber_table
body:
Per the spec, name objects cannot contain unescaped delimiters, which include angle, curly and square brackets (currently missing).

I've attached a sample input PDF with a valid, correctly escaped name object. When appending this to a new PdfWriter the output is invalid (incorrectly escaped):

```
>>> w = PdfWriter()
>>> w.append('nameobj.pdf')
>>> w.write('out.pdf')
```
[out.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20173760/out.pdf)
[nameobj.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20173761/nameobj.pdf)

step_plan.summary: Add explicit hex-escape mappings for the angle, square and curly bracket delimiters to NameObject.renumber_table and ensure NameObject.renumber() consistently applies those mappings so names containing '<', '>', '[', ']', '{', '}' are serialized safely and consistently with the parser.

steps.operation: Add missing hex-escape mappings to the NameObject renumber_table
steps.reason: NameObject.delimiter_pattern treats '<', '>', '[', ']', '{', '}' as name delimiters when parsing, but renumber_table did not include mappings for these characters. Adding explicit mappings ("<": b"#3C", ">": b"#3E", "[": b"#5B", "]": b"#5D", "{" : b"#7B", "}" : b"#7D") makes serialization escape them consistently with the reader and produces valid PDF output.
steps.side_effects: Serializing NameObject instances that contain these characters will now produce escaped sequences (changes output bytes). This may break byte-level output comparisons or signatures in downstream code/tests. No changes to public API surface are required, but consumers that rely on previously unescaped bytes will need to adapt. Run/adjust unit tests and full test suite to account for changed serialization. Other parts of the code that expect names to be emitted unescaped should be reviewed.

steps.operation: Review and make renumber() explicitly consult renumber_table for each byte and preserve existing fallback behavior
steps.reason: Although renumber() already uses renumber_table, explicitly ensuring it iterates over input bytes and uses renumber_table.get(byte, fallback) makes the behavior clear and robust after the renumber_table change. This ensures the newly added delimiter mappings are actually applied and documents the intended fallback (hex-escape any unmapped byte). A small refactor or inline comment here also aids future maintainers and reduces the chance of regressions.
steps.side_effects: If renumber() is refactored to be more explicit about fallback hex-escaping, behavior should remain compatible, but any subtle differences in edge-case escaping must be validated. Update/extend unit tests to validate renumber() output for names containing the newly escaped delimiters and run PDF round-trip tests. Changes are internal to the serializer API; callers of NameObject.renumber() (if any) may observe different bytes for previously-unescaped characters, so integration tests should be run.
